specification.topic-statement: The internal function wrappers expose a fixed set of application-internal operations through the link-seam mechanism so tests can inject failures or substitute behavior without modifying production code.

scope.in-scope: The seventeen internal application operations exposed as link seams, the void-pointer type erasure used in debug-mode declarations, the build-mode selection between inline (release) and weak-symbol (debug) form, and the transparent passthrough contract for each wrapped call.
scope.boundary:
The MOCKABLE attribute and the build-mode dispatch strategy are shared infrastructure.
In release builds the wrappers are static inline and carry their concrete type casts inline. In debug builds they are weak symbols whose declarations use void pointers to avoid pulling application headers into consumer translation units.

Each wrapper forwards to the real implementation. The semantics of paths translation, database operations, scrollback, REPL rendering, agent state transitions, request building, HTTP multiplexing, tool execution, template processing, and token counting are documented in separate specifications for those subsystems.

field.name: structured context arguments
field.note: opaque pointers in the debug-mode header declarations; cast to concrete types inside the .c implementation

field.name: structured context arguments
field.note: fully typed in the release-mode inline definitions; no casting visible to callers

behavior.type-erasure-strategy:
In debug builds, the wrapper declarations in the header use generic opaque pointer types in place of the concrete structured types. This avoids requiring consumers of the wrapper header to also include the full set of application domain headers. The .c implementation file includes the concrete headers and performs the type cast internally before forwarding to the real function.
In release builds, the inline definitions include the concrete headers and carry the casts directly, so callers see the full types and no erasure occurs.
A test that calls a wrapper in debug mode sees a void pointer signature, while production code compiled in release mode sees a fully typed signature. The two compilation modes present different call sites to the compiler.
behavior.uri-translation-seams: Two path-translation operations are wrapped: converting an application-scheme URI to a filesystem path, and converting a filesystem path to an application-scheme URI. Both accept an allocator context, an opaque paths configuration handle, an input string, and an output string pointer. Both return a result value. All arguments are forwarded unchanged after casting the opaque handle to the concrete type.
behavior.external-tool-execution-seam: The external tool execution operation is wrapped. It accepts an allocator context, a tool path string, an agent identifier string, a JSON arguments string, an output pointer for the child process ID, and an output pointer for the result string. Returns a result value. All arguments are forwarded unchanged.
behavior.database-seams:
Two database operations are wrapped.
Database initialization accepts an allocator context, a connection string, a data directory path, and an output pointer for the opaque database context. Returns a result value.
Message insertion accepts an opaque database context handle, a session identifier, an agent UUID string, a message kind string, a content string, and a JSON data string. Returns a result value.
Both forward all arguments unchanged after casting the opaque handle.
behavior.scrollback-seam: The scrollback line-append operation is wrapped. It accepts an opaque scrollback handle, a text buffer, and a byte length. Returns a result value. All arguments are forwarded unchanged after casting.
behavior.repl-rendering-seam: The REPL frame rendering operation is wrapped. It accepts an opaque REPL context handle and returns a result value. The handle is cast to the concrete type and forwarded.
behavior.agent-provider-seam: The agent provider retrieval operation is wrapped. It accepts an opaque agent handle and an output pointer for the opaque provider handle. Returns a result value. Both handles are cast to concrete types internally.
behavior.request-building-seam: The request build operation is wrapped. It accepts an allocator context, an opaque agent handle, an opaque tool registry handle, and an output pointer for the opaque request handle. Returns a result value. All opaque handles are cast to concrete types internally.
behavior.http-multi-seams:
Two HTTP multiplexer operations are wrapped.
The creation operation accepts a parent allocator context and an output pointer. The underlying call returns a result value; when that result indicates success, the ok field of the result is written to the output pointer before the result is returned. This means the caller receives the created handle via the output pointer rather than directly from the result value.
The info-read operation accepts an opaque HTTP multiplexer handle and an opaque logger handle. It returns nothing. Both handles are cast to concrete types internally.
The HTTP multi creation wrapper unpacks the result's ok pointer into a separate out-parameter, diverging from the raw underlying function signature. All other wrappers forward their result values without modification.
behavior.agent-tool-loop-seams:
Three operations supporting the tool execution loop are wrapped.
Starting tool execution accepts an opaque agent handle and returns nothing.
Querying whether the loop should continue accepts a const opaque agent handle and returns an integer (nonzero means continue, zero means stop).
Submitting a tool loop continuation accepts an opaque REPL context handle and an opaque agent handle and returns nothing.
All three forward their arguments unchanged after casting.
behavior.agent-state-seams:
Two agent state-management operations are wrapped.
Message addition accepts an opaque agent handle and an opaque message handle. Returns a result value. Both handles are cast to concrete types internally.
Transitioning the agent to idle accepts an opaque agent handle and returns nothing. The handle is cast to the concrete type internally.
behavior.template-processing-seam: The template processing operation is wrapped. It accepts an allocator context, a template text string, an opaque agent handle, an opaque configuration handle, and an output pointer for the opaque result handle. Returns a result value. All opaque handles are cast to concrete types internally.
behavior.token-counting-seam: The HTTP-based token counting operation is wrapped. It accepts an allocator context, a URL string, an API key string, a request body string, an output pointer for the response string, and an output pointer for the HTTP status code. Returns a result value. All arguments are forwarded unchanged.
